entry.text: The pipe burst under the road sometime in the last decade and nothing has been done about it since, because nothing can be done about it since. The street has become a river — slow, brown, pressurised, carrying the pre-war city's full biological legacy downstream at a walking pace. Debris rides the current: furniture, car doors, a mattress moving with the determined pace of something that has somewhere to be. The smell is total and absolute. The current is strong enough to carry you. There's a rusted car door wedged against a fire hydrant at the edge, large enough to stand on.

choices.label: Ride the car door downstream — balance and steer

surf_success.text: You cover three blocks in ninety seconds, riding the car door with your arms out for balance, navigating around submerged lamp posts, and dismounting onto a loading dock that presents itself at exactly the right moment. You are covered in what you're covered in. You are, measurably, much further down the road than you were.

wade_through.text: Hip-deep and slow-moving, but you make it across without going under. The current pushes but doesn't take you. You emerge on the other side comprehensively saturated and wanting a fire.

surf_failure.text: The car door catches a submerged obstacle and pitches you face-first into the current. You surface one block downstream, choking, covered in the full catalogue of what this water contains. The 'Filthy' condition is almost certainly the least of your current problems, but it is the one you can name.

submerged.text: A submerged step drops you from hip-deep to chest-deep without warning. You go under briefly and inhale something you cannot uncatalogued. The bacterial content of your current situation is significant.
